purpose: Canonical authenticated Customer Portal dashboard for MMG Knowledge Subscription plan status, entitlement progress, package windows, title selections, deliveries, recovery actions, My Library access, and membership controls.

progress_contract.ownership_authority: active non-revoked ownership grants keyed by customer_id + asset_id

current_package_contract.recovery_action: Resolve this package through Customer Service or an authorized recovery workflow
current_package_contract.countdown_source: server-provided closesAt rendered by the customer-facing runtime

resource_links.manage_membership: Shopify customer account URL supplied by Liquid or the authenticated server adapter

storefront_implementation.integration_rule: Add the dashboard to the existing Customer Portal without replacing service-project, support, authentication, navigation, or other established portal workflows.
storefront_implementation.progressive_enhancement: Private subscription data requires JavaScript and the authenticated endpoint; existing portal navigation and support links remain available without JavaScript.

customer_states.loading: Announce that the secure membership dashboard is loading and disable private content.
customer_states.unauthenticated: Direct the customer to the Shopify account sign-in flow without exposing subscription data.
customer_states.no_subscription: Present membership discovery and preserve access to public portal resources.
customer_states.active: Show the complete plan, progress, package, timeline, and resource dashboard.
customer_states.inactive: Show the latest membership record and direct the customer to renew or manage the account.
customer_states.recovery_required: Prominently direct the customer to the governed recovery path.
customer_states.error: Preserve the page shell, announce the error, and direct the customer to refresh or contact support.

security_contract: Derive customer identity exclusively from the authenticated server session.
Never accept customer ID, subscription ID, cycle capacity, package status, ownership, or entitlement totals from Liquid or browser input.
Return private no-store responses and vary on the authenticated session cookie.
Do not expose Shopify provider contract IDs, internal delivery dispatch IDs, delivery package references, ownership-grant IDs, or entitlement-event payloads.
Use parameterized SQL for customer and cycle lookups.
Keep all package mutations in the Knowledge Library Picker or protected Delivery Window Controller; the initial dashboard endpoint is read-only.
Treat links as server-configured or Shopify-generated routes rather than browser-authoritative commerce state.

subsequent_components: Live Shopify provisioning and end-to-end deployment

release_gates: The authenticated Customer Portal session is mapped to the canonical customer ID server-side.
The dashboard endpoint is routed through the deployed MMG runtime with private no-store headers.
Both entitlement database migrations are applied and the production PostgreSQL connection is active.
Live Shopify subscription contracts are reconciled into the entitlement tables.
The Delivery Window Controller creates and advances package windows in production.
At least two verified subscription-selectable assets exist and display complete square thumbnails.
The existing live Customer Portal source is captured before integration.
The dashboard is inserted without removing current portal workflows or weakening authenticated boundaries.
Mobile, keyboard, screen-reader, countdown, inactive-membership, recovery, delivery, and error-state QA passes.
Repository and production observability can distinguish dashboard API failures from unrelated page-shell workflows.
